title: T-004 — Migration: allergen tables (item profiles, manufacturing_op additions, contamination risk)
prompt:
# T-004 — Migration: allergen tables (item profiles, manufacturing_op additions, contamination risk)

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §5.4, §10.1, §10.4, §10.5
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Three allergen-domain tables with RLS, source/intensity/confidence enums, contamination risk grid.

allergen_code is TEXT with no hard FK — soft reference to reference_tables.allergens_reference (02-SETTINGS) so EU-14 + org custom rows are valid. manufacturing_operation_name aligned with reference_tables.manufacturing_operations.row_key (00-FOUNDATION §9.1). line_id and machine_id reference 02-SETTINGS.production_lines/machines (FK if present, else soft).

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- db/migrations/0xxx_allergens.sql [create]
- db/schema/allergens.ts [create]
- tests/db/allergens.migration.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- Cascade rule logic (T-024)
- Override audit emit (T-025)
- Allergen UI (T-046..050)

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration applied, when inserting an item_allergen_profiles row with invalid intensity, then CHECK rejects.
2. Given two orgs share the same allergen_code, when RLS is active per org, then rows from the other org are not visible.
3. Given allergen_contamination_risk row with risk_level not in enum, then insert fails.

## Test strategy
- RED tests for CHECK constraints and RLS isolation.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/db/allergens.migration.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not introduce hard FK on allergen_code — keep schema-driven extensibility per ADR-028.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
